what_it_does: This file is a draft noticeboard document that frames an independent iOS designer perspective on the phone-annotation system, presented as a deliberately non-converging, block-addressable commentary that can be attached to any part.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, board:// address, type=document, source=claude_code, state=draft, channel=dragnet-development, author_session=ch-3mpkjg3r
The title is 'iOS designer — perspective on the phone-annotation system'
An 'order' array lists ten board:// item addresses, implying a curated sequence of related items
History shows two transitions: filed (null→draft) and an order edit (edit→order) with note 'perspective blocks'
Body content is only a framing sentence: 'An independent research perspective (one lens, deliberately non-converging). Block-addressable — comment on any part.'
standouts: Explicit framing as an independent, non-converging research lens
Order list of ten related board items
Draft state with minimal body content
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter and markdown body
Uses board:// URI identifiers for cross-item ordering
Declares its role/perspective explicitly in the body
concerns: The document is a stub: it announces a perspective but contains no actual perspective blocks or design claims
The relationship of the ten ordered items to this perspective is not explained
'phone-annotation system' is named but not defined or scoped within the file
notes: A draft noticeboard stub that positions an iOS designer's independent, block-level commentary on a phone-annotation system and links to ten related items.
questions: What specific iOS design perspective will be recorded here?
What is the phone-annotation system and which parts need annotation?
How do the ten ordered items relate to this perspective?
Will the 'perspective blocks' be stored in this file or in separate items?
What does 'deliberately non-converging' mean operationally in this ledger?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to provide an independent iOS designer perspective on the phone-annotation system; actual content is a framing placeholder with an ordered list of related items and no substantive perspective blocks yet.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a separable, addressable design-research artifact that lets an iOS designer attach per-block commentary to a system without collapsing that commentary into a single consensus view.
novelty: The explicit framing of a perspective as a 'deliberately non-converging' lens that is block-addressable, rather than a convergent design decision or review.
contribution: Provides a structured placeholder for an iOS designer viewpoint and an ordered set of related board items, preserving the relationship between a perspective and the items it may annotate without merging it into a canonical design record.
summary_for_embedding: Draft noticeboard document titled 'iOS designer — perspective on the phone-annotation system' containing a framing statement for an independent, block-addressable, non-converging perspective and an ordered list of ten related board items.
intention_for_embedding: To capture an iOS designer's independent, per-block commentary on the phone-annotation system while keeping that viewpoint separate from convergent design decisions.
